candidate_lesson.lesson_text: When the 30-indicator consensus shows conviction=100% on a pair (AUDUSD: 14/30 bear) but green_matches=0 against historical memory, treat the signal as direction-only and gate size down — high live conviction with zero historical regime match is an unvalidated extrapolation, not a confirmed edge.
candidate_lesson.mechanism: Indicator consensus measures present-bar agreement, but green_matches=0 means no analogous historical bar produced this configuration with a known good outcome. Institutional flow that creates durable trends leaves repeated, recognizable footprints; a 100%-conviction reading with no historical twin is more consistent with a transient liquidity sweep or one-sided positioning that mean-reverts than with a committed trend. The hist_hr=70% is a base rate from a DIFFERENT (matched) population, so applying it to an unmatched live state overstates expected win rate.
candidate_lesson.testable_rule: if consensus_conviction >= 0.90 and green_matches == 0: size_multiplier *= 0.6  # require historical match before full size on extreme-conviction reads
candidate_lesson.expected_impact: Reduces variance of high-conviction entries that lack historical confirmation; expected Sharpe +0.02 to +0.04 via avoided drawdown on false-extreme signals, win rate stabilization on AUDUSD/EURUSD shorts.
candidate_lesson.evidence_from_harvest: Current live state: AUDUSD conv=100% green_matches=0 hist_hr=70%; EURUSD conv=58% green_matches=0 hist_hr=66%. Both flash strong directional reads with zero historical regime matches — the hr figures are borrowed from non-matching populations and cannot be trusted as live expectations.

reflection.system_health_note: Zero trades in 7 days with Regime=UNKNOWN and TradingView showing 0 signals — the system is in an observation freeze, so no decision-log lessons are possible yet and harvest-only inference is the correct fallback.
